title: api: GET /content/posts/: emit static dist/content/posts.json shadow for @tryghost/content-api consumers
body:
Ghost behavior: `GET /ghost/api/content/posts/?key=...` returns `{ posts: Post[], meta: { pagination } }` of *all* published posts (paginated, default `limit=15`).

Nectar gap: we emit zero JSON; only HTML routes. External SPAs / mobile apps / Zapier / `@tryghost/content-api` SDK cannot read the catalog.

Proposal: during `nectar build`, after `planRoutes`, write `dist/content/posts.json` containing the full posts array projected to the Ghost Content API shape (fields per `src/content/model.ts:Post` minus internal `prev`/`next` link refs; serialize `tags`, `authors`, `primary_tag`, `primary_author` as embedded objects so `?include=tags,authors` is satisfied by default). One file, full dump — pagination shards handled in a follow-up issue. Gate behind `[components.content_api] enabled = true` (default on).

title: api: GET /content/posts/: emit paginated shards dist/content/posts/page/N.json matching meta.pagination shape
body:
Ghost behavior: paginated with `?page=N&limit=L`; response carries `meta.pagination = { page, limit, pages, total, next, prev }`. Default `limit=15`, max `limit=15` for posts (and `limit=all` for full dump).

Nectar gap: no pagination shards.

Proposal: for each `limit` in `[15, 'all']` (configurable list), emit:
- `dist/content/posts.json` (page 1, default limit)
- `dist/content/posts/page/2.json`, `.../3.json`, ...
- `dist/content/posts/all.json` (limit=all)

Each payload includes `meta.pagination` with `page`/`limit`/`pages`/`total`/`next`/`prev` (next/prev are page numbers, not URLs, per Ghost). Document that arbitrary `?limit=N&page=M` query combos won't be honored — only the canonical shards.

title: api: GET /content/posts/:id/ and /content/posts/slug/:slug/: emit per-post JSON shadows
body:
Ghost behavior: `/content/posts/:id/` and `/content/posts/slug/:slug/` return `{ posts: [Post] }` (single-element array).

Nectar gap: no per-post JSON; we only have the HTML at `/:slug/`.

Proposal: alongside `dist/<slug>/index.html`, emit `dist/content/posts/slug/<slug>.json` and `dist/content/posts/<id>.json`. Same Post projection as the index shard. Allows `@tryghost/content-api`'s `posts.read({slug})` to work read-only against a static host.

title: api: GET /content/pages/: emit dist/content/pages.json + per-page shards
body:
Ghost behavior: `/content/pages/` returns the static-page collection with same envelope as posts. Distinct from posts (filtered server-side, `page=true`).

Nectar gap: pages are loaded into `ContentGraph.pages` but never serialized.

Proposal: emit `dist/content/pages.json` (full list, paginated if >15) and `dist/content/pages/slug/<slug>.json`. Same field projection as Post minus post-only fields (`featured`, `prev`/`next`).

title: api: GET /content/tags/: emit dist/content/tags.json with count.posts populated
body:
Ghost behavior: returns all public tags with `count.posts` populated when `?include=count.posts` is set. Default order `?order=name asc`.

Nectar gap: `Tag.count.posts` is already on the model but never written out.

Proposal: emit `dist/content/tags.json` with the full public tag list (drop `visibility: internal`), each tag carrying `count.posts`. Also `dist/content/tags/slug/<slug>.json` for individual lookup.

title: api: GET /content/authors/: emit dist/content/authors.json with count.posts
body:
Ghost behavior: returns all authors with `count.posts` when `?include=count.posts`.

Nectar gap: no authors JSON; `count.posts` not even computed on `Author` (model lacks the field — see separate issue).

Proposal: add `count.posts` to `Author` derived from `posts.filter(p => p.authors.some(a => a.id === author.id))`, then emit `dist/content/authors.json` and `dist/content/authors/slug/<slug>.json`. Drop email/internal-only fields per Content API contract.

title: api: Author model lacks count.posts (blocker for /content/authors/ shadow)
body:
`src/content/model.ts:Author` has no `count` field. Ghost Content API exposes `count.posts` per-author when `?include=count.posts` is requested. Theme `{{#get "authors"}}` blocks would render without post counts, and the static JSON shadow can't be added without first extending the model.

Fix: add `count: { posts: number }` to `Author`, populate during content graph build in `src/content/loader.ts` after posts are linked.

title: api: GET /content/settings/: emit dist/content/settings.json mirroring @site context
body:
Ghost behavior: `/content/settings/` returns the site-wide settings — `title`, `description`, `logo`, `icon`, `cover_image`, `accent_color`, `locale`, `timezone`, `url`, `navigation`, `secondary_navigation`, `meta_title`, `meta_description`, `og_image`, `twitter_image`, `members_signup_access`, `paid_members_enabled`, `firstpromoter_account`, etc.

Nectar gap: site config exists at `nectar.toml [site]` and is exposed via `@site` context but never serialized.

Proposal: emit `dist/content/settings.json` shaped as `{ settings: SiteData }` matching Ghost's envelope. Members-related fields hardcoded to `false`/empty (see /content/tiers/ stub issue).

title: api: GET /content/tiers/: emit empty dist/content/tiers.json stub for SDK compatibility
body:
Ghost behavior: `/content/tiers/` returns membership tiers. Nectar is SSG-only with no membership.

Nectar gap: SDK consumers calling `api.tiers.browse()` get 404 / network error today.

Proposal: emit `dist/content/tiers.json = { tiers: [], meta: { pagination: { page:1, limit:15, pages:0, total:0, next:null, prev:null } } }` so the SDK call resolves to an empty array cleanly. Document in `GHOST_COMPATIBILITY.md` that members are out of scope.

title: api: GET /content/newsletters/: emit empty dist/content/newsletters.json stub
body:
Ghost behavior: `/content/newsletters/` returns newsletter definitions; tied to members/email features.

Nectar gap: out of scope, but absence causes SDK 404s.

Proposal: emit empty-array shadow identical in shape to /content/tiers/ stub. Cheap, removes a class of consumer breakage.

title: api: meta.pagination shape — define canonical projector helper
body:
Ghost pagination envelope (must match exactly for `@tryghost/content-api` to parse):

```
{ "meta": { "pagination": { "page": 1, "limit": 15, "pages": 3, "total": 42, "next": 2, "prev": null } } }
```

Nectar gap: nothing emitting this today.

Proposal: introduce `src/build/api/pagination.ts` exporting `paginate<T>(items: T[], limit: number, page: number): { items: T[]; pagination: GhostPagination }`. All collection shadow emitters (posts/pages/tags/authors) share this helper. `limit='all'` produces `pages: 1, total: items.length, next: null, prev: null`.

title: api: filter (NQL) — emit pre-baked tag/author filtered shards instead of supporting arbitrary NQL
body:
Ghost behavior: `?filter=tag:foo+published_at:>'2024-01-01'` uses NQL — a non-trivial query language (`tag:`, `tags:[a,b]`, `featured:true`, `visibility:public`, date comparators, AND `+` / OR `,`).

Nectar gap: static files can't run NQL.

Proposal: don't implement NQL. Instead emit **pre-baked shards** for the common filters that themes and SDKs hit:
- `dist/content/posts/tag/<slug>.json` — `filter=tag:<slug>` equivalent
- `dist/content/posts/author/<slug>.json` — `filter=author:<slug>` (deprecated Ghost name) and `authors:<slug>`
- `dist/content/posts/featured.json` — `filter=featured:true`

Document that arbitrary `?filter=` will not work; recommend client-side filtering of the full `posts.json`. Issue a follow-up to add a tiny NQL→predicate shim in a future `nectar serve` if needed.

title: api: filter NQL — document explicit non-support and 404 strategy
body:
Consumers will inevitably pass `?filter=...` to a static host. They'll get the unfiltered full dump (query string stripped) silently — which is *worse* than a 404 because results look correct but contain too many items.

Proposal: ship a `_headers` / `_redirects` snippet (Netlify/Cloudflare format) that 404s any request to `/content/*.json?filter=*` or similar. Better yet, document a `nectar.config.ts` flag `api.strict_query_rejection = true` that, when running behind a worker, returns 404 on unknown query params.

Minimum for this issue: README/`GHOST_COMPATIBILITY.md` section listing which query params are honored vs ignored.

title: api: ?fields= projection — document non-support; emit full records only
body:
Ghost behavior: `?fields=title,slug` returns a projected subset.

Nectar gap: static JSON always returns the full record.

Proposal: don't implement field projection. Document that the static API always returns full objects; clients should project in-memory. Cite `@tryghost/content-api`'s behavior — it doesn't strictly require server-side projection.

title: api: ?include=authors,tags — always embed by default (no opt-out cost)
body:
Ghost behavior: `?include=tags,authors` decides whether each post carries embedded tags/authors arrays vs id-only refs.

Nectar gap: model already has full `tags[]` / `authors[]` arrays.

Proposal: always emit posts with `tags`, `authors`, `primary_tag`, `primary_author` embedded. Adds ~hundreds of bytes per post but avoids needing client-side joins. Document as a deliberate divergence: `?include=` is ignored, includes are always on.

title: api: ?include=count.posts — pre-populate on tags/authors shadows unconditionally
body:
Ghost requires explicit `?include=count.posts` on tags/authors endpoints to get post counts.

Proposal: in the static JSON, always include `count.posts`. Saves a request param consumers tend to forget, and the data is free at build time.

title: api: ?order= — emit canonical orderings only (posts: published_at DESC; tags: name ASC; authors: name ASC)
body:
Ghost supports arbitrary `?order=field+direction`.

Proposal: static shards use Ghost's defaults — posts `published_at desc`, tags `name asc`, authors `name asc`. Document non-support of arbitrary `?order=`. Clients can re-sort in memory.

Verify defaults match Ghost's actual defaults before shipping.

title: api: ?formats=html,plaintext — both always emitted; ?formats= ignored
body:
Ghost: `?formats=html,plaintext,mobiledoc` toggles which content representations come back. Default is `html` only; `plaintext` and `mobiledoc` are opt-in.

Nectar gap: `Post` model already has `html` + `plaintext`. Mobiledoc/Lexical not supported (per GHOST_COMPATIBILITY.md).

Proposal: always emit both `html` and `plaintext` (cheap, already computed). Omit `mobiledoc`/`lexical` (would need source-of-truth content representation we don't keep). Document that `?formats=mobiledoc` returns no field — clients should fall back to `html`.

title: api: CORS — emit dist/_headers granting Access-Control-Allow-Origin: * on /content/*.json
body:
Static JSON served from a different origin than the consumer SPA will be blocked by CORS unless headers are explicit.

Proposal: emit `dist/_headers` (Netlify format) and `dist/_headers.cf` (Cloudflare Pages format, identical syntax) granting:
```
/content/*
  Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *
  Access-Control-Allow-Methods: GET, HEAD, OPTIONS
  Access-Control-Allow-Headers: Content-Type, Authorization
  Cache-Control: public, max-age=300
```
Make configurable via `[components.content_api] cors_origin = "*"`.

title: api: CORS — Apache .htaccess + nginx snippet alternatives
body:
Not every host supports Netlify/Cloudflare's `_headers`. Self-hosters on nginx/Apache need CORS too.

Proposal: ship docs section + sample snippets at `docs/deploy/cors-{nginx,apache,caddy}.md` showing how to set the same headers. Optional flag `[components.content_api] emit_htaccess = true` writes a `.htaccess` to `dist/content/`.

title: api: Content API key (?key=) — accept and ignore; document stub for SDK init compatibility
body:
Ghost Content API requires `?key=<26 hex chars>`. `@tryghost/content-api` requires a key at SDK init or it throws.

Nectar gap: we don't auth; static files are world-readable. But SDK consumers still need a key value to pass.

Proposal: document that the SDK can be initialized with any 26-char hex placeholder (e.g. `nectarnectarnectarnectar00`) and Nectar will ignore it. Ship an example snippet in `docs/SDK.md`. Optionally add a build-time sanity check that emits a dummy key file like `dist/content/.key` for tooling that scrapes it.

title: api: Content API URL versioning — emit under /ghost/api/content/ path too (not just /content/)
body:
Ghost's canonical URL prefix is `/ghost/api/content/posts/`. `@tryghost/content-api` constructs URLs as `${host}/ghost/api/content/<resource>/?key=...&v=v5.0`.

Nectar gap: I proposed `dist/content/posts.json` for brevity, but the SDK won't hit that path.

Proposal: emit shadows under `dist/ghost/api/content/<resource>.json` to match the SDK's path. Optionally also under shorter `/content/` via a symlink or duplicate write. Verify by pointing `@tryghost/content-api` at a `nectar build` output.

title: api: ?v=5.0 version query — strip and document; emit a single representation
body:
Ghost SDK appends `?v=v5.0` (or `?v=v3.0` for legacy). Static hosts ignore query strings.

Proposal: only emit the latest (v5) representation. Document that older `?v=` values won't get a different file. Future-proof by versioning the *path* (e.g. `/ghost/api/content/v5/posts.json`) if we ever ship a v6 schema differential.

title: api: trailing-slash handling — emit both /posts.json and /posts/ (directory index) variants
body:
Ghost URLs are `/content/posts/` (trailing slash). With static hosting, `/content/posts/` is a directory request, so the file must be `dist/content/posts/index.json`. But `@tryghost/content-api` may not append `index.json`.

Proposal: investigate exactly what URL the SDK requests. Likely it's `/posts/?key=...` (trailing slash). Emit `dist/ghost/api/content/posts/index.json` AND ensure a `_redirects` rule rewrites `/posts/` → `/posts/index.json` if the host doesn't auto-resolve directory indexes for JSON.

Acceptance: `await new ContentAPI({...}).posts.browse()` returns posts against a `nectar build` deployed to plain S3.

title: api: SDK smoke test fixture — add tests/sdk/content-api.test.ts running @tryghost/content-api against dist/
body:
We have no test verifying that an actual Ghost SDK consumer can talk to a `nectar build` output. Without this we'll regress on URL paths, payload shape, pagination keys, etc.

Proposal: in `tests/sdk/content-api.test.ts`, build the example site, spin a `Bun.serve` over `dist/`, point `new ContentAPI({ url, key, version: 'v5.0' })` at it, and assert:
- `posts.browse()` returns array + pagination meta
- `posts.read({slug})` returns single post
- `tags.browse()` returns tags with `count.posts`
- `authors.browse()` returns authors
- `settings.browse()` returns site settings

Gate behind a dev dep on `@tryghost/content-api`.

title: api: search — map /content/search/ to Pagefind output OR provide a flat search.json fallback
body:
Ghost has a `/search/` endpoint (and `sodo-search` integration). It returns hits across posts/pages/tags/authors.

Nectar gap: no search emission.

Proposal: emit two artifacts:
1. `dist/content/search.json` — flat `{ posts: [{id, slug, title, excerpt, url}], tags: [...], authors: [...] }` suitable for client-side fuzzy search (lunr/Fuse/minisearch). Cheap.
2. Optional Pagefind integration — `[components.search] engine = "pagefind"` runs `pagefind` over `dist/` post-build, emitting `dist/pagefind/*`. Document the consumer JS snippet.

Document that Ghost's exact `/search/` endpoint shape is NOT replicated; this is a divergent search API.

title: api: search — sodo-search drop-in: emit posts list at /content/posts.json (already covered) is sufficient for sodo
body:
`sodo-search` (Ghost's official search widget) fetches `/ghost/api/content/posts/?limit=all&fields=id,slug,title,excerpt,url,updated_at,visibility&order=updated_at%20desc`. If we already emit `posts/all.json` with the right fields + path, sodo-search works without code change.

Proposal: verify by dropping sodo-search into the example theme and pointing it at a built site. File follow-up issues for any field/shape mismatch found.

title: api: oembed — explicitly skip and document
body:
Ghost has `GET /oembed/?url=...` that proxies to oembed providers (YouTube, Twitter, etc.) and is used by the editor to embed cards. Build-time SSG has no editor, no need for the endpoint.

Proposal: do not emit. Add a one-line note in `GHOST_COMPATIBILITY.md` under "things we don't handle".

title: api: webhooks — explicitly skip; document for tooling consumers
body:
Ghost Admin API has webhooks. Static SSG has nothing to webhook from.

Proposal: document non-support. If a CI/CD integration needs build-completion notifications, point users at GitHub Actions / their host's deploy hooks.

title: api: Admin API — skip entirely; document that Nectar is read-only
body:
Ghost Admin API (`/ghost/api/admin/*`) is write-capable: posts CRUD, tags CRUD, members, integrations, etc.

Proposal: do not emit. Document loudly in README that `@tryghost/admin-api` is *not* compatible with Nectar — users author content via Markdown files + git, not via the Admin API. Link to the `nectar import` command for the migration path.

title: api: Admin API — provide a one-way Admin→Markdown import (already exists?) and document it as the migration path
body:
We have `src/ghost/import.ts`. Verify it covers: posts, pages, tags, authors, settings, images. If anything is missing, file separate issues.

Proposal for this issue: docs page `docs/MIGRATION.md` explaining the import flow and listing what's lost (members, custom integrations, snippets, custom fields not in frontmatter).

title: api: AMP — Ghost emits /post-slug/amp/; decide and either emit or 410
body:
Ghost themes ship an `amp.hbs` template; Ghost auto-generates `/post-slug/amp/`. Google has deprecated AMP ranking signals; many Ghost sites disable it.

Proposal: opt-in `[components.amp] enabled = false` default. When enabled, emit `dist/<slug>/amp/index.html` rendering through theme's `amp.hbs` (or a built-in fallback). When disabled, document that consumers should not link to `/amp/` paths.

title: api: per-tag RSS at /tag/:slug/rss/ — already filed under feeds; cross-reference
body: Per task instructions, RSS variants (per-tag, per-author) are already filed separately. Cross-link from this API audit: any consumer expecting `/tag/foo/rss/` is hitting an RSS endpoint, not the Content API, but `@tryghost/content-api` consumers may also want `/content/posts/?filter=tag:foo` to reflect the same set — covered by the tag-filtered shard issue above. No new work in this issue; ensure label hygiene only.

title: api: ?key= validation — emit a key registry file dist/.well-known/ghost-content-keys.json (optional)
body:
Some Ghost integrations sniff allowed keys. For full SDK compatibility we don't need this, but a `.well-known` file documenting that any key is accepted may help integrations.

Proposal: low priority; mention in docs. Don't implement until a real consumer reports breakage.

title: api: post.html field — verify it matches Ghost's serialization (no Ghost-specific card markup leaking)
body:
Ghost's `post.html` includes Ghost-specific markup: `<figure class="kg-card kg-image-card">`, `<!--kg-card-begin: html-->` comments, member-only paywall splits, etc.

Nectar emits Markdown→HTML plain. Consumers reading `post.html` from the static API may rely on Ghost card classes (e.g. to apply CSS for kg-image-card layouts).

Proposal: document the divergence in `GHOST_COMPATIBILITY.md`. Optionally add `[components.markdown] emit_kg_classes = true` that wraps images in `<figure class="kg-card kg-image-card">` and similar. Phase 2: a markdown plugin that renders inline `{% card %}` directives into kg-card HTML.

title: api: post.excerpt vs post.custom_excerpt vs post.plaintext — verify Ghost's exact rules
body:
Ghost's Content API rules:
- `excerpt` = `custom_excerpt` if set, else first ~50 words of `plaintext`
- `custom_excerpt` = the field set in the editor, may be null
- `plaintext` = HTML stripped

Nectar: `Post` model has all three. Need to verify `loader.ts` populates them with Ghost's exact precedence and word count (~50 words, not chars).

Proposal: audit `src/content/loader.ts:normalizePost` against Ghost's source. File a fix if mismatched.

title: api: post.reading_time — verify Ghost's algorithm (275 wpm + image adjustment)
body:
Ghost's `reading_time` = `ceil(word_count / 275) + image_seconds/60`, where each image adds 12s decreasing to 3s. See `@tryghost/helpers/lib/utils/reading-time.js`.

Nectar: `Post.reading_time` exists but algorithm not verified.

Proposal: port Ghost's exact formula; add a test fixture comparing against `@tryghost/helpers`. Note: existing self-audit already flags word_count CJK bug — fix that first.

title: api: post.access / post.visibility — emit visibility but document access is always 'public'
body:
Ghost: `post.access` boolean (true if current member can read), `post.visibility` (`public`/`members`/`paid`/`tiers`). Without a session, Content API returns posts based on the key's tier permissions.

Nectar: no members, no session, no paywall.

Proposal: always emit `access: true`, preserve `visibility` from frontmatter for completeness, but filter out `visibility: members`/`paid` posts from the public JSON by default (configurable). Document.

title: api: post.email_only / post.email — set false/null on all posts
body:
Ghost has email-only posts (`email_only: true`, only sent to subscribers, never on the public site). Plus `post.email` object with stats.

Nectar: no email, no members.

Proposal: emit `email_only: false`, `email: null` on every post for shape compatibility with the SDK's TypeScript types.

title: api: post.send_email_when_published — emit false constant
body: Internal Ghost field. SDK types include it. Static API should emit `false` for shape parity.

title: api: post.uuid — emit a stable UUID per post (separate from id)
body:
Ghost has both `id` (24-hex ObjectId) and `uuid` (RFC4122) on posts. Some consumers key off uuid.

Nectar: only `id` (we may be generating ObjectId-shaped or slug-shaped). Need to verify and add `uuid` (deterministically derived from content path + a project namespace, so it's stable across builds).

Proposal: add `uuid` to `Post`/`Page` derived via `uuid v5` with namespace = site URL, name = slug.

title: api: post.id format — verify it's a 24-char hex ObjectId or document divergence
body:
Ghost IDs are MongoDB ObjectIds (24 hex). Some SDK consumers regex-validate.

Nectar: check `src/content/loader.ts` — if we're using slugs or random IDs, document divergence or generate deterministic 24-hex from a hash of slug+published_at.

Proposal: deterministic hex(sha1(slug+published_at)).slice(0,24).

title: api: tag.url — verify it matches Ghost's /tag/:slug/ convention and config.url is prefixed
body:
Ghost emits absolute URLs in API responses (`https://site.com/tag/foo/`). Theme helpers expect this.

Nectar: `Tag.url` exists; verify it's absolute (with `[site].url` prefix) and ends with trailing slash.

Proposal: spot-check in `loader.ts`; add a test.

title: api: author.url — same audit as tag.url
body: Mirror of the tag.url issue. Authors' URLs in the Content API are absolute `https://site.com/author/:slug/`. Verify.

title: api: post.url — verify absolute, trailing-slash, respects custom permalink config
body: Ghost has configurable permalink structures via routes.yaml. We don't support routes.yaml (separate issue elsewhere). For the API shadow, ensure `post.url` is absolute with trailing slash. If the user configures a permalink prefix later, the JSON shadow must update.

title: api: meta.pagination.next/prev as numbers, not strings
body:
Ghost returns `next` and `prev` as numbers (or null), not strings. SDK parses them as numbers. Easy to get wrong with JSON serializers.

Proposal: ensure the pagination helper emits numbers. Add a test that does `typeof body.meta.pagination.next === 'number' || body.meta.pagination.next === null`.

title: api: cache-control headers per resource — short TTL for posts, longer for tags/authors
body:
Ghost API typically returns `Cache-Control: no-cache` or short TTLs. For a static deploy we want longer TTLs to leverage CDN.

Proposal: in `dist/_headers`:
- `/content/posts/*` → `max-age=300` (5 min)
- `/content/tags/*`, `/content/authors/*`, `/content/settings/*` → `max-age=3600` (1 hour)
- `/content/tiers/*`, `/content/newsletters/*` → `max-age=86400` (1 day, basically empty)

Configurable via `[components.content_api.cache]`.

title: api: 404 envelope — emit Ghost-shaped error JSON for missing slug requests
body:
Ghost 404s return:
```json
{ "errors": [{ "message": "Resource not found error, cannot read post.", "context": "...", "type": "NotFoundError", "details": null, "property": null, "help": null, "code": null, "id": "..." }] }
```

Static host returns its own 404 HTML. SDK will fail to JSON-parse.

Proposal: emit a `dist/content/404.json` with Ghost's error envelope. Hosts that support custom 404 status (Netlify `_redirects`, Cloudflare Pages) can route `/content/*` 404s to this file with the right status code. Document the hosting config.

title: api: HEAD/OPTIONS support — document via _headers preflight
body:
Browsers fire OPTIONS preflight on cross-origin requests with auth headers. Many static hosts auto-handle OPTIONS, but not all.

Proposal: in the CORS `_headers` block, ensure preflight returns `Access-Control-Max-Age: 86400` to cache the preflight. Document host-specific quirks (S3 needs explicit OPTIONS in bucket policy).

title: api: build flag --emit-content-api / config gate [components.content_api]
body:
All the JSON shadow work above should be gated, off-by-default during the unstable phase, on-by-default once stable.

Proposal: add to `nectar.toml`:
```
[components.content_api]
enabled = true
paginate_limit = 15
emit_per_resource_shards = true
emit_filtered_shards = ["tag", "author", "featured"]
cors_origin = "*"
```
Wire through `src/build/pipeline.ts` similar to existing `components.rss` / `components.sitemap` gating.

title: api: emit dist/content/posts/index.json *and* dist/content/posts.json — host directory-index variance
body:
Hosts differ on whether `/foo/` serves `/foo/index.html` or 404s without `index.html`. JSON has no equivalent default. We must emit redundantly:
- `dist/content/posts.json` (file at non-trailing-slash request)
- `dist/content/posts/index.json` (file at trailing-slash directory request, if host serves *.json indexes)

Proposal: emit both. Or pick one + ship a `_redirects` rewriting `/posts/` → `/posts.json`. Document.

title: api: TypeScript types — publish @nectar/content-api-types matching the emitted JSON shape
body:
Consumers writing TypeScript want types for the JSON they fetch. `@tryghost/content-api` ships its own types but they include fields we omit (members, mobiledoc).

Proposal: publish `@nectar/content-api-types` exporting `NectarPost`, `NectarPage`, `NectarTag`, `NectarAuthor`, `NectarSettings`, `NectarPagination`. Re-export from `src/content/model.ts` with API-only fields stripped/added. Lowest priority — wait for first external consumer to ask.

title: api: document non-API endpoints we DO emit (sitemap.xml, rss.xml, robots.txt)
body: Cross-link from `docs/GHOST_COMPATIBILITY.md` API section: which JSON we emit, which we don't. List existing emissions (sitemap, rss) and proposed (content/*.json). Saves consumers from wondering.

title: api: emit dist/.well-known/ghost.json with site metadata (api version, generator) for service discovery
body:
Optional service-discovery file consumers could probe to detect a Nectar-served Ghost-compatible site vs a real Ghost.

```json
{ "generator": "nectar", "version": "0.x", "ghost_api_version": "v5.0", "endpoints": { "content": "/ghost/api/content/" } }
```

Consumers can use this to opt into Nectar-aware behavior (e.g. skip admin calls).

title: api: members-only fields on Post — strip from public JSON (paywall_html, members content)
body:
Ghost posts can carry `<!--members-only-->` markers; Content API returns truncated content for non-members. Without members, we either emit full content or strip the marker.

Proposal: parse `<!--members-only-->` in Markdown, drop everything after it from `html` and `plaintext` for `visibility: members`/`paid` posts, OR (simpler) reject those visibilities at build with a clear error since Nectar doesn't support paywalls.

title: api: posts include excerpt of first paragraph if custom_excerpt unset — verify Ghost's word count (50 words default)
body:
Ghost: auto-excerpt is *50 words* of plaintext, not characters, not full paragraph. Easy to get wrong.

Proposal: audit `src/content/loader.ts` excerpt generation; ensure it's `plaintext.split(/\s+/).slice(0, 50).join(' ')` (or `Intl.Segmenter`-based for CJK). Add test.

title: api: post.featured boolean — verify it's filterable via the featured shard
body: Crosscheck: tag/author shards proposed above. `featured.json` should contain `posts.filter(p => p.featured)`. Trivial; just confirm it's wired.

title: api: ?absolute_urls=true — Ghost option to rewrite relative URLs in html to absolute
body: Ghost Content API supports `?absolute_urls=true` which rewrites relative `` to absolute. Useful for RSS readers / external consumers. Proposal: emit `html` with absolute URLs by default in the Content API shadow (cheap, one-time rewrite at build). Document that the `?absolute_urls` query param is ignored because output is always absolute. Follow-up: keep relative URLs in the on-page HTML for portability; only rewrite in the JSON shadow.

title: api: image URLs — Ghost's /content/images/size/wWIDTH/path responsive variants — document non-support
body:
Ghost's Image API resizes on-the-fly: `/content/images/size/w600/2024/01/foo.jpg`. Themes use this via `{{img_url ... size="w600"}}`.

Nectar gap: covered separately as a helper. For the API shadow: image URLs in `post.html` / `post.feature_image` won't have responsive variants unless the user pre-generates them.

Proposal: document. Optional follow-up: `[components.images] sizes = [600, 1200, 2000]` pre-generates resized assets at build via `sharp`. Out of scope for this audit; file separately if not already.

title: api: example consumer — ship docs/EXAMPLE_SPA.md showing @tryghost/content-api against a Nectar build
body:
Reference implementation. A 50-line React/Vue/Svelte snippet that fetches `posts.browse()` against a Nectar deployment, demonstrating the path setup and key stub. Acts as living documentation and a regression target.

Proposal: write the doc, link from README. Bonus: add to `example/` as a runnable subdir.
